name: spherical
comment: Spherical (elliptic) palette: regular {3,4,5,6,8,10}-gons at D=120 (3-degree units). closure=positive-defect admits vertex figures whose interior angles sum to STRICTLY LESS than a full turn (angular defect > 0). Discrete Gauss-Bonnet then forces the glued closed map to have positive total defect (720 degrees = 4*pi), i.e. a sphere (Euler characteristic 2), instead of the Euclidean torus (defect 0). This is the geometry-agnostic-engine TEST only: it runs solver+pruner (combinatorial), develop is skipped (the Z[zeta12]+translation-lattice developer cannot realize a curved finite surface; {T1,T2,Seed} is a wallpaper fundamental domain). Tiles chosen so every Archimedean face angle lands on the D=120 grid: interior angles {60,90,108,120,135,144} have gcd 3 = 360/120. Not pinned (no legacy gate).
